8.text: That is, the leader of the exiles returning to Judah

9.text: Or basins ; twice in this verse

18.text: Jozadak is a variant of Jehozadak ; see 1 Chronicles 6:14.

38.text: See LXX; Hebrew And Bani, and Binnui, (and) Shimei,

44.text: Or and they sent them away with their children

10.text: Bani is a variant of Binnui ; see Nehemiah 7:15.

18.text: Jorah is a variant of Hariph ; see Nehemiah 7:24.

2.text: Parallel text at Nehemiah 7:7 accompanied by Zerubbabel, Jeshua, Nehemiah, Azariah, Raamiah, Nahamani, Mordecai, Bilshan, Mispereth, Bigvai, Nehum, and Baanah

20.text: Gibbar is a variant of Gibeon ; see Nehemiah 7:25.

21.text: Literally the sons ; here and in verses 25, 26, 33, and 34

24.text: Azmaveth is a variant of Beth-azmaveth ; see Nehemiah 7:28.

40.text: Hodaviah is a variant of Hodevah ; see Nehemiah 7:43.

43.text: Hebrew The Nethinim ; also in verses 58 and 70

44.text: Siaha is a variant of Sia ; see Nehemiah 7:47.

46.text: Alternate MT reading (see also Nehemiah 7:48); the other alternate reads Shamlai

50.text: Nephusim is a variant of Nephushesim ; see Nehemiah 7:52.

52.text: Bazluth is a variant of Bazlith ; see Nehemiah 7:54.

55.text: Peruda is a variant of Perida ; see Nehemiah 7:57.

57.text: Ami is a variant of Amon ; see Nehemiah 7:59.

59.text: Addan is a variant of Addon ; see Nehemiah 7:61.

69.text: Or 61,000 gold drachmas ; that is, approximately 1,129.7 pounds or 512.4 kilograms of gold coins
5,000 minas is approximately 3.14 tons or 2.85 metric tons of silver.

11.text: Forms of the Hebrew chesed are translated here and in most cases throughout the Scriptures as loving devotion ; the range of meaning includes love , goodness , kindness , faithfulness , and mercy , as well as loyalty to a covenant .

2.text: Jozadak is a variant of Jehozadak ; also in verse 8; see 1 Chronicles 6:14.

4.text: That is, Sukkot, the autumn feast of pilgrimage to Jerusalem; also translated as the Feast of Booths or the Feast of Shelters and originally called the Feast of Ingathering (see Exodus 23:16 and Exodus 34:22).

9.text: Hebrew sons of Judah ; that is, most likely, sons of Yehudah , another name for Hodevah or Hodaviah ; see Ezra 2:40 and Nehemiah 7:43.

10.text: Aramaic Osnappar , another name for Ashurbanipal
Aramaic beyond the River ; also in verses 11, 16, 17, and 20

14.text: Literally because the salt of the palace is the salt we eat

7.text: The original text of Ezra 4:8 through Ezra 6:18 is in Aramaic.

10.text: Aramaic the names of the men at their heads

2.text: Jozadak is a variant of Jehozadak ; see 1 Chronicles 6:14.

3.text: Aramaic the governor beyond the River ; also in verse 6

4.text: LXX and Syriac; Aramaic Then we told them,

11.text: Literally shall be made a dunghill because of this

15.text: Adar is the twelfth month of the Hebrew lunar calendar, usually occurring within the months of February and March.

22.text: That is, the seven-day period after the Passover during which no leaven may be eaten; see Exodus 12:14–20.

3.text: The house was to be approximately 90 feet or 27.4 meters in height and width; Syriac (see also 1 Kings 6:2) It is to be sixty cubits long, twenty cubits wide, and thirty cubits high .

6.text: Aramaic governor beyond the River ; similarly in verses 8 and 13

11.text: The original text of Ezra 7:12–26 is in Aramaic.

21.text: Aramaic beyond the River ; also in verse 25

22.text: 100 talents is approximately 3.77 tons or 3.42 metric tons of silver.
100 cors is approximately 624 bushels or 22,000 liters (probably about 19.2 tons or 17.4 metric tons of wheat).
100 baths is approximately 580 gallons or 2,200 liters of wine.
Or 100 baths of oil ; that is, approximately 580 gallons or 2,200 liters

10.text: Some LXX manuscripts (see also 1 Esdras 8:36); Hebrew from the descendants of Shelomith, the son of Josiphiah

14.text: LXX, Syriac, and an alternate MT reading; the other alternate reads Zabbud .

16.text: Elnathan appears three times in verse, either as a repetition for emphasis or as a record of multiple men with this name.

17.text: Hebrew the Nethinim ; also in verse 20

26.text: 650 talents is approximately 24.5 tons or 22.2 metric tons of silver.
100 talents is approximately 3.77 tons or 3.42 metric tons of silver articles.
100 talents is approximately 3.77 tons or 3.42 metric tons of gold.

27.text: Or worth 1,000 drachmas ; that is, approximately 18.5 pounds or 8.4 kilograms of gold

36.text: A satrap was a Persian official.

5.text: Some LXX manuscripts (see also 1 Esdras 8:32); Hebrew from the descendants of Shecaniah, the son of Jahaziel
